command_result.summary: Model files transferred via rsync, service installed and running. Scoring endpoint verified end-to-end.

steps_completed.detail: 41 files, 531 MB transferred at ~1.45 MB/s. All model files, tokenizer, config, and result JSONs present on Hetzner.
File ownership changed to psq:psq on all files in /opt/psychology-agent/safety-quotient/models/psq-student/
Changed User=ubuntu to User=psq in psq-server.service
Copied service to /etc/systemd/system/, daemon-reload, enabled, started. Required additional step: service file was in deploy/ but not symlinked to systemd — installed manually.

Initial start failed: @huggingface/transformers@3.8.1 bundled onnxruntime-node@1.21.0 conflicted with top-level onnxruntime-node@1.24.2 (VERS_1.21.0 symbol missing). Fixed by removing nested node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/node_modules/onnxruntime-node/, letting transformers use the top-level 1.24.2.
curl http://localhost:3000/health returned {status: ok, model: psq-student, calibration_version: isotonic-v1-2026-03-06, ready: true}

POST /score with test text returned full machine-response/v3. Composite 36/100, 5/10 dims above threshold, 84ms inference. energy_dissipation=2.98 (strongest threat signal, correct for overwhelm text).

issues_encountered.resolution: Removed nested onnxruntime-node@1.21.0 from @huggingface/transformers/node_modules/. Top-level 1.24.2 serves both packages. This fix will not survive npm install — needs a package.json override or postinstall script for durability.
Service file existed in deploy/ but was not copied to /etc/systemd/system/. Copied manually. Future deployments should include this step.

claims.text: model.onnx SHA256 matches between Chromabook source and Hetzner destination: bc5d7f29...d833c52a. model_quantized.onnx also matches: 28c9a950...2f128239.
claims.confidence_basis: sha256sum run on both machines, hashes compared.

claims.text: PSQ scoring endpoint is live at http://178.156.229.103:3000. Health check and scoring test both passed. Full machine-response/v3 output with calibrated scores, hierarchy, limitations, and claims.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct curl verification: /health returned ok, /score returned valid JSON with expected dimensions and composite.

claims.text: The onnxruntime-node conflict fix (removing nested copy) will not survive npm install. A durable fix is needed: either pin @huggingface/transformers to a version compatible with onnxruntime-node@1.24.2, or add an npm overrides entry, or add a postinstall script.
claims.confidence_basis: npm dependency resolution will re-create the nested node_modules on next install. 0.90 not 1.0: possible the override in package.json already covers this.

action_gate.gate_condition: PSQ endpoint live — psychology-agent can now set PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL via wrangler secret
action_gate.gate_note: Ready for: wrangler secret put PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL with value http://178.156.229.103:3000. One remaining issue: onnxruntime-node fix is fragile (won't survive npm install). Recommend adding npm override before next deployment.

epistemic_flags: onnxruntime-node fix is a workaround, not a durable solution. Next npm install on Hetzner will recreate the conflict.
Endpoint is HTTP (not HTTPS). CF Worker → Hetzner traffic is unencrypted unless CF Tunnel is used.
Port 3000 is bound to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces). Should be firewalled to only accept connections from CF edge or localhost if using CF Tunnel.
